_comment: A JPL Horizons FETCH SPEC, not an ephemeris. It is the query that produces
this directory, and for each source either loads the already-cached CSV from
.cache/ephemeris/ or queues the Horizons request that creates it. The state
vectors themselves are that CSV — machine-local, gitignored, refetchable.
This file used to also carry Artemis II's trajectory colours, sampling rates
and spacecraft marker sizes, and MissionPlugin spawned all of it into any
scene that declared a celestial body. That visualisation config is now USD
(assets/missions/artemis_2_mission.usda) and is opted into per scene. What is
left here is the part that was never scene config: how to obtain the numbers.
If committed kernels ever replace the fetch, the right format is CCSDS OEM
(502.0-B KVN) or SPICE SPK — at which point this spec is replaced by the
asset itself rather than a recipe for downloading it.
